title: What changes about my day when the wealth lands
subtitle: A journal prompt set for examining how wealth would actually fit into the shape of your everyday life.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Day 26 of the MONEY program. Use to move from abstract wealth aspiration to the specific details of how money would land inside the life you are already living.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 26 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798276631448).

practice.whenToUse: Use when wealth still feels like an abstract future event rather than something that could fit inside your actual daily routine.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Day 26 of the MONEY program. The prompts work at the level of the ordinary day — the morning, the commute, the Tuesday afternoon. The aim is to make wealth concrete by placing it inside the routines and structures you already have. Twenty minutes, no editing.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What current routines or habits already reflect a well-supported, harmonious life?
Find the evidence. Even small things count.
What part of me believes wealth might complicate or disrupt my life? Where does that belief come from?
What changes about my day when the wealth lands?
Be specific. Morning to night. What is different?
Write the things you wouldn't change even with more money. These are the things you are protecting.
How could wealth actually simplify my life rather than complicate it?
Name three specific things that would become easier.
Adapted from Day 26 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 4 daily prompts of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
